name: Barbecued Pork Strips
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the marinade:
- 2 tablespoons light soy sauce
- 2 tablespoons Chinese rice wine or dry sherry
- 2 tablespoons sugar
- 2 tablespoons minced garlic
- 2 tablespoons brown bean curd
- 2 tablespoons hoisin sauce
- 1 tablespoon 5-spice powder
2. Cut the pork butt into two halves.
3. Cut each half into 3/4-inch strips.
4. Place the strips in a bowl and pour over the marinade, mixing well to coat them thoroughly.
5. Marinate at room temperature for 3 hours, or overnight in the refrigerator.
6. Remove the pork from the marinade and baste the strips with the malt-sugar mixture.
7. Arrange the pork strips on a rack above a large pan filled with water to a depth of 1/4 inch.
8. Roast at 350°F for 45 minutes, basting occasionally with the malt sugar or honey.
9. Increase the heat to 425°F and roast for an additional 20 minutes until the pork is cooked through.
10. Allow the pork to cool, then slice into 1/2-inch pieces.
11. Serve hot as a main course with vegetables (serves 4-6) or cut smaller for appetizers (8-10 servings).
